# Valor Dashboard — Feature Roadmap

**Generated by 7 Sonnet brainstorming agents + Opus synthesis | March 31, 2026**

---

## THE PITCH

Your dashboard today is a webhook logger. It catches appointments, creates tasks, and shows you what happened. That is table stakes. This roadmap turns it into the **operational nerve center** for your entire settlement team — the one screen every officer opens in the morning and never closes. By the end of Phase 2, your team will be managing cases, spotting problems, and tracking performance without ever logging into IRS Logics directly for routine work. By the end of Phase 3, the system will be doing work *for* them — catching stuck cases, handling no-shows, and writing activity logs automatically so nothing falls through the cracks.

The strategy is deliberate: Phase 1 lays plumbing that every later feature depends on (case detail pages, activity logging, error visibility). Phase 2 builds the daily-use tools that make officers faster (search, alerts, analytics). Phase 3 adds the cross-API automations that eliminate manual work entirely. We are *not* building a billing system, a fax portal, or a scheduling tool. IRS Logics already does those things. We are building the **decision layer** on top of IRS Logics — the thing that tells your team what needs attention right now and handles the busywork so they can focus on settling cases.

The total scope is roughly 18 features across 9+ weeks. Each phase ships something usable. Nothing in Phase 1 is speculative; nothing in Phase 4 is critical. If you stopped after Phase 2, you would already have a tool no off-the-shelf product could replicate. Everything after that is compounding advantage.

---

## COMBINED FEATURES (Merges)

Before the roadmap, these overlapping proposals were merged:

| Merged Feature | Source Proposals |
|---|---|
| **Case Deep-Dive Page** | UX #2 + COMMS #1 (Activity Timeline) + COMMS #8 (Health Snapshot) + FINANCE #2 (Billing Health Score). One page at `/case/[caseId]`. |
| **Stuck Case Alerts** | CASES #4 + AUTOMATION #5. One cron, one alert system. |
| **Error & Failure Center** | ANALYTICS #4 (Error Rate Monitor) + UX #3 (Failure Inbox). Analytics + action in one page. |
| **Pipeline Funnel** | ANALYTICS #3 + CASES #8 + FINANCE #8. One funnel: appointments → cases → tasks. |
| **Officer Performance View** | ANALYTICS #2 (Leaderboard) + ANALYTICS #6 (Fairness) + FINANCE #7 (Scorecard). One page. |

---

## PHASE 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-2)

Low-effort, high-impact. Dependencies for everything that follows.

### 1.1 — Automated Case Activity Logging
> When the webhook creates a task, auto-write an Activity entry on the IRS Logics case.

- **Agents:** COMMS #5 (7/7 unanimous vote)
- **APIs:** `POST /V4/CaseActivity/Activity`
- **Complexity:** Low (~20 lines added to webhook handler)
- **Why Phase 1:** One function addition. Makes IRS Logics self-documenting. Officers who never touch the dashboard still benefit. Every later feature that reads activities benefits from richer data.
- **Dependencies:** None

### 1.2 — Case Deep-Dive Page (v1)
> Detail page at `/case/[caseId]` — case info, assigned officers, status, task history from `task_logs`.

- **Agents:** UX #2 (5/7 vote), COMMS #1, COMMS #8
- **APIs:** `GET /V4/Case/CaseInfo?CaseID={id}&details=setofficerid,attorneyid,casemanagerid,caseworkerid`
- **Complexity:** Medium
- **Why Phase 1:** Every later feature needs somewhere to link TO. Alerts, search results, activity feeds — they all dead-end without a case page. v1 is read-only: metadata + task_logs history.
- **Dependencies:** None

### 1.3 — Error & Failure Center (v1)
> `/errors` page showing failed webhooks with error categorization, retry button, and trend sparkline.

- **Agents:** ANALYTICS #4 + UX #3 (3/7 vote)
- **APIs:** Supabase `task_logs` (status != 'success'), existing webhook for retry
- **Complexity:** Low-Medium
- **Why Phase 1:** Data already exists. Failures are currently invisible. Retry button resubmits original payload. This is operational hygiene that prevents silent failures from compounding.
- **Dependencies:** None

### 1.4 — GHL Conversation Summary Panel
> Display `conversations_ai_summary` and `conversations_ai_transcript` (already in `task_logs`) on the case page.

- **Agents:** COMMS #4
- **APIs:** None new — data already in `task_logs`
- **Complexity:** Low
- **Why Phase 1:** Pure frontend work. Zero API calls. Officers get instant pre-call context about what was discussed before the appointment.
- **Dependencies:** Case Deep-Dive Page (1.2)

---

## PHASE 2: Core Value (Weeks 3-5)

The features that make the dashboard indispensable for daily use.

### 2.1 — Global Case Search
> `/search` page with multi-field search (email, phone, last name + phone, CaseID) linking to Case Deep-Dive.

- **Agents:** CASES #5, UX #4
- **APIs:** `FindCaseByEmail`, `FindCaseByPhone`, `FindCaseByLastNameAndPhone`, `CaseInfo`
- **Complexity:** Medium
- **Why Phase 2:** Replaces the current `/lookup`. Officers can find cases with whatever info the client gives over the phone.
- **Dependencies:** Case Deep-Dive (1.2)

### 2.2 — Case Deep-Dive Page (v2): Activity Timeline + Billing Summary
> Extend the case page with IRS Logics activity feed and billing health badge (contracted/collected/outstanding).

- **Agents:** COMMS #1 (Timeline), FINANCE #2 (Billing Health)
- **APIs:** `GET /V4/CaseActivity/Activity`, `GET /V4/Billing/CaseBillingSummary`
- **Complexity:** Medium
- **Why Phase 2:** Activity timeline + billing summary = everything an officer needs to prep for a call without leaving the dashboard.
- **Dependencies:** Case Deep-Dive (1.2)

### 2.3 — Stuck Case Alerts
> Daily cron detecting cases with no activity in 14+ days. Surfaces on `/alerts` page. Auto-posts pinned activity note.

- **Agents:** CASES #4 + AUTOMATION #5 (4/7 vote)
- **APIs:** `GET /V4/Report/ActivityReport`, `POST /V4/CaseActivity/Activity` (Pin: true)
- **Complexity:** Medium-High
- **Why Phase 2:** First proactive feature — the system tells officers what needs attention. Compliance-critical for a tax firm. Uses Activity API already integrated in 2.2.
- **Dependencies:** Activity API (2.2), Case Deep-Dive (1.2)

### 2.4 — Task Volume Trend Chart + Appointment Heatmap
> Two chart components on Overview: daily task creation time-series + GitHub-style heatmap of appointment booking times.

- **Agents:** ANALYTICS #1 + CALENDAR #4 (3/7 vote)
- **APIs:** Supabase `task_logs` (aggregation queries only)
- **Complexity:** Low-Medium
- **Why Phase 2:** Zero new API calls. All data in task_logs. Adds the first charts to a dashboard with zero visualizations. Trend = "are we getting busier?" Heatmap = "when should we staff up?"
- **Dependencies:** `recharts` npm package

### 2.5 — Officer Performance View
> `/officers/performance` page with per-officer task counts, case_officer vs round_robin breakdown, fairness drift indicator.

- **Agents:** ANALYTICS #2, ANALYTICS #6, FINANCE #7
- **APIs:** Supabase `task_logs` (group by officer, assignment_method)
- **Complexity:** Low-Medium
- **Why Phase 2:** All data exists. Answers "is round-robin fair?" and "who's getting the most work?" Management visibility.
- **Dependencies:** None

---

## PHASE 3: Power Features (Weeks 6-8)

Cross-API automations that eliminate manual work.

### 3.1 — Appointment No-Show Handler
> New GHL webhook trigger on "No-Show" → auto-creates follow-up task on IRS Logics case + suggests available rebooking slots.

- **Agents:** AUTOMATION #7 + CALENDAR (3/7 vote)
- **APIs:** GHL Webhook (new trigger), `POST /V4/Task/Task`, optionally `GET /V4/Appointment/GetAvailableSlots`
- **Complexity:** Medium
- **Why Phase 3:** Second automation in the system. No-shows are a real revenue leak. Same webhook plumbing, new trigger.
- **Dependencies:** Stable webhook handler (Phase 1), GHL Workflow config

### 3.2 — Pipeline Funnel Visualization
> `/analytics/funnel` showing: GHL appointments → cases found → tasks created → (future: paying clients).

- **Agents:** ANALYTICS #3, CASES #8, FINANCE #8 (3/7 vote)
- **APIs:** Supabase `task_logs` (counts by status), optionally `CaseBillingSummary`
- **Complexity:** Medium
- **Why Phase 3:** The executive view. "How efficient is our intake pipeline?" First two stages from task_logs, payment stage is Phase 4.
- **Dependencies:** Billing API integration (2.2) for full funnel

### 3.3 — Case Notes Composer
> Form on Case Deep-Dive page to post activity notes to IRS Logics with Pin and Popup options.

- **Agents:** CASES #7, COMMS #2
- **APIs:** `POST /V4/CaseActivity/Activity` (CaseID, Subject, Comment, Pin, Popup)
- **Complexity:** Low-Medium
- **Why Phase 3:** First write action officers can take from the dashboard. Turns the case page from read-only into a workspace.
- **Dependencies:** Case Deep-Dive v2 (2.2)

### 3.4 — Overdue Task Escalation Engine
> Cron checking for overdue tasks (past DueDate, StatusID=0). Auto-creates escalation tasks assigned to case manager.

- **Agents:** AUTOMATION #3
- **APIs:** `GET /V4/Task/TaskOrEvent`, `POST /V4/Task/Task`, `GET /V4/Case/CaseInfo?details=casemanagerid`
- **Complexity:** Medium-High
- **Why Phase 3:** "System works while you sleep." Same cron pattern as Stuck Case Alerts but for tasks specifically.
- **Dependencies:** Stuck Case Alerts (2.3) cron pattern

---

## PHASE 4: Premium (Week 9+)

Build once the core is proven and daily-use is established.

### 4.1 — "My Cases" Personal Dashboard
> Officer-specific home screen at `/my-cases` with their assigned cases, upcoming appointments, personal stats.

- **Agents:** UX #1
- **APIs:** `CasesByStatus` filtered by officer, `task_logs` filtered
- **Complexity:** Medium-High
- **Why Phase 4:** Requires auth/identity (knowing which officer is viewing). Dashboard has no login system today. High value but high prerequisite cost.
- **Dependencies:** Auth system (not yet built)

### 4.2 — Auto Case Creation from GHL
> When webhook finds no IRS Logics case, auto-create one instead of returning 404.

- **Agents:** AUTOMATION #1
- **APIs:** `POST /V4/Case/Case`
- **Complexity:** High
- **Why Phase 4:** Changes the webhook's fundamental contract. Compliance implications — needs business rules discussion with firm leadership.
- **Dependencies:** Business rules definition, API validation

### 4.3 — Exportable Reports (CSV/PDF)
> One-click export of activity logs, officer stats, and pipeline data.

- **Agents:** UX #6, ANALYTICS #5
- **APIs:** None new
- **Complexity:** Low-Medium
- **Why Phase 4:** Nice-to-have. The dashboard is the report. Export is a convenience.
- **Dependencies:** Analytics pages (2.4, 2.5)

### 4.4 — Case Status Sync (IRS Logics → GHL Tags)
> Nightly cron syncing case statuses to GHL contact tags.

- **Agents:** AUTOMATION #2
- **APIs:** `CaseInfo`, GHL Contact Update API
- **Complexity:** High
- **Why Phase 4:** Two-way sync is fragile. High value for marketing/sales in GHL, but settlement team lives in dashboard.
- **Dependencies:** Stable GHL API (currently has 401 issues)

---

## KILLED IDEAS

| Feature | Agent | Why Cut |
|---|---|---|
| **Kanban Board** | CASES #2, UX #8 | Looks impressive, miserable to maintain. IRS Logics is the status system of record. Stuck Case Alerts solves the real problem without duplicating data. |
| **IRS Fax Sender** | COMMS #3 | Compliance-sensitive action with cover sheet requirements and POA attachments. Use IRS Logics native fax — dashboard should not own compliance-critical workflows. |
| **Fax Number Directory** | COMMS #7 | A static reference table is not a software feature. Put it in a Google Sheet. |
| **Document Upload Tracker** | COMMS #6 | Document management UI is massive scope (previews, versioning, categorization). IRS Logics already handles this. |
| **Invoice Generator** | FINANCE #6 | Billing write actions from a secondary system = audit nightmare. Read-only billing visibility is the right call. |
| **Payment Recorder** | FINANCE #4 | Same — writing payments from a non-primary system into billing system of record is dangerous. |
| **Amortization Viewer** | FINANCE #5 | Niche. Billing summary in Case Deep-Dive v2 covers 90% of the need. |
| **Two-Way Appointment Sync** | CALENDAR #5 | Two-way sync between two third-party APIs is the most fragile architecture possible. |
| **Availability Gate** | CALENDAR #1 | Round-robin assigns tasks, not appointments. Availability gating needs a rethinking of the assignment model, not a bolt-on. |
| **Command Palette (Cmd+K)** | UX #7 | Power-user feature for a dashboard with 4 pages and 13 users. Premature. |
| **Weekly Digest Email** | ANALYTICS #5 | Nobody reads automated emails. The dashboard IS the report. Export (4.3) covers on-demand needs. |
| **Conflict Detector** | CALENDAR #6 | GHL handles scheduling natively. Re-implementing adds no value. |
| **Availability Widget** | CALENDAR #7 | Tasks are not appointments. Checking slots is irrelevant to current assignment model. |
| **Collections At-Risk Board** | FINANCE #3 | Requires deep billing API integration. If billing summary reveals the need, revisit later. |
| **Nightly Reconciliation** | AUTOMATION #8 | Comparing all GHL contacts vs all IRS Logics cases = expensive and noisy. Pipeline funnel answers the meaningful version. |
| **Payment Milestone Auto-Booking** | AUTOMATION #4 | Too many moving parts. Stuck Case Alerts + billing summary give officers info to decide manually. |
| **Document Auto-Request on Milestone** | AUTOMATION #6 | Assumes reliable status change detection (doesn't exist). Doc requests are case-specific. |

---

## DEPENDENCY GRAPH

```
Phase 1
  1.1 Activity Logging ──────────────────────────────────┐
  1.2 Case Deep-Dive v1 ──┬── 2.1 Global Search          │
  1.3 Error Center         │── 2.2 Case Deep-Dive v2 ─┬── 2.3 Stuck Case Alerts
  1.4 Conversation Panel   │                           │── 3.3 Case Notes Composer
                           │                           │── 3.2 Pipeline Funnel
                           │                           └── 3.4 Escalation Engine
                           │
Phase 2                    │── 2.4 Charts (independent)
                           └── 2.5 Officer Performance (independent)

Phase 3
  3.1 No-Show Handler (independent, needs GHL config)

Phase 4
  4.1 My Cases (needs auth)
  4.2 Auto Case Creation (needs business rules)
  4.3 Export (needs analytics pages)
  4.4 Status Sync (needs stable GHL API)
```

---

## PRE-FLIGHT VALIDATION

Before Phase 1 starts, test these APIs:
1. `POST /V4/CaseActivity/Activity` — confirm field names, required params, Pin/Popup flags
2. `GET /V4/CaseActivity/Activity?CaseID={id}` — confirm response shape and chronological ordering
3. Resolve GHL API 401 issue (noted in project memory) — Phase 3.1 depends on it

---

## TECH NOTES

- **Charts:** Add `recharts` — lightweight, React-native, covers 2.4, 2.5, 3.2
- **Cron expansion:** Extend existing `/api/cron/process-pending` with sweep functions for Stuck Case Alerts (2.3) and Escalation (3.4)
- **New Supabase table:** `alerts` — `case_id`, `alert_type`, `created_at`, `resolved_at`, `resolved_by`
- **Billing cache:** `billing_cache` table with TTL for Phase 2.2 CaseBillingSummary results
